Moving from Pennsylvania to Tampa Bay

  1. Home
  2. /Moving From
  3. /Moving from Pennsylvania to Tampa Bay

Why Pennsylvanians Are Moving to Tampa Bay

Pennsylvania's 3.07% flat income tax is low compared to neighbors like New Jersey and New York, but many PA cities add local earned income taxes (1–3.75% in Philadelphia). Property taxes are above national averages, winters are cold and gray, and the cost of living in the Philadelphia and Pittsburgh suburbs continues to climb. Tampa Bay offers zero income tax, no city taxes, warmer weather, and a lifestyle upgrade that has steadily drawn PA families south.

This page covers the statewide Pennsylvania perspective. If you're specifically in the Philadelphia area, also check our Moving from Philadelphia guide for city-specific details.

Cost of Living Comparison

Category Pennsylvania Tampa Bay Difference
Median home price $290,000 $400,000 +38%
State income tax 3.07% flat 0% -100%
Local income tax 1–3.75% 0% -100%
1BR apartment rent $1,100–$1,800 $1,500–$1,800 +0–35%
Groceries 103 (index) 103 (index) Similar
Homeowners insurance $900–$1,500/yr $2,500–$5,000/yr Much higher
Heating costs $1,500–$3,000/yr $0 -100%

Tampa Bay is more expensive for housing and insurance. The tax savings, elimination of heating costs, and lifestyle upgrade justify it for most transplants. For Pittsburgh-area families looking at affordable Tampa Bay counties like Hernando or Polk, the housing gap narrows significantly.

Tax Savings Breakdown

Household Income PA State + Local Tax FL State Tax Annual Savings
$75,000 $3,050–$5,100 $0 $3,050–$5,100
$100,000 $4,070–$6,820 $0 $4,070–$6,820
$150,000 $6,105–$10,230 $0 $6,105–$10,230

Higher end includes Philadelphia's 3.75% wage tax. Lower end is suburban PA with minimal local tax.

Pennsylvania has an inheritance tax (4.5–15% depending on beneficiary relationship). Florida has none.

Culture Shock and Lifestyle Differences

Central PA / Pittsburgh vs. Eastern PA

Pennsylvania is really two states culturally. Pittsburgh and central PA share midwestern sensibilities — friendly, humble, sports-obsessed. Philadelphia and its suburbs are more Northeast — direct, fast-paced, opinionated. Tampa Bay absorbs both types well, but the adjustment is different. Pittsburghers feel instantly at home with Tampa Bay's friendly vibe. Philadelphians may need a month to decompress.

The Outdoor Lifestyle Shifts

Pennsylvania has beautiful hiking — the Appalachian Trail, Poconos, and Allegheny National Forest. Tampa Bay is flat but trades mountain trails for beaches, springs, kayaking, and fishing. If your outdoor identity is trail-based, you'll need to build a new routine around water-based activities. Most people find the swap rewarding.

Winter Disappears

Pennsylvania winters range from "manageable" in the southeast to "brutal" in the north and west. Lake-effect snow in Erie, ice storms in Harrisburg, gray skies everywhere. Tampa Bay eliminates all of it. The first time you spend January in shorts grilling in your backyard, you'll wonder why anyone lives in a cold climate voluntarily.

Best Tampa Bay Areas for PA Transplants

  • Pittsburgh suburbs → Brandon / Riverview in Hillsborough County — affordable family suburbs, friendly community
  • Lehigh Valley / Lancaster → Wesley Chapel or Land O' Lakes in Pasco County — growing suburbs, new construction
  • State College / central PA → Lakeland in Polk County — small-city vibe, college-town energy (thanks to USF and Florida Southern)
  • Poconos / rural PA → Spring Hill or Brooksville in Hernando County — affordable, quieter pace

The Move Itself

Pennsylvania to Tampa Bay is 900–1,200 miles depending on your location — 14–18 hours of driving.

  • Professional movers: $3,500–$7,000
  • PODS Moving & Storage — $2,500–$5,000
  • U-Haul — $1,200–$2,800
  • Route from Pittsburgh: I-79 → I-77 → I-26 → I-95 → I-4 (or I-77 → I-26 → I-95 → I-10 → I-75)
  • Route from eastern PA: I-95 South through D.C. corridor

Setting Up Your New Life

  1. Auto insurance — Policygenius for Florida rates.
  2. Driver's license and registration — 30-day deadline. See FL guides.
  3. Internet — Spectrum Internet primary. T-Mobile Home Internet alternative.
  4. Home security — ADT Home Security or SimpliSafe.
  5. Handyman — Best Bay Services — Handyman & Home Services for settling in.
  6. Homestead exemption — January 1 – March 1. See guide.

FAQ

Is Sheetz or Wawa in Florida?

Wawa has expanded into Florida with locations in the Tampa Bay area. Sheetz has not. Score one for the eastern PA crowd.

Can I keep my Steelers/Eagles loyalty?

Absolutely. Tampa Bay has bars for every NFL fan base. Steelers bars are particularly plentiful — the Steelers Nation travels well. Eagles fans are also well-represented thanks to the Philly-to-Tampa pipeline and Phillies spring training in Clearwater.

How does the healthcare compare?

Tampa Bay has world-class facilities: Tampa General (Level 1 trauma), Moffitt Cancer Center (nationally ranked), Johns Hopkins All Children's. See our Healthcare guide. It rivals Pittsburgh's UPMC system and Philadelphia's hospital corridor.
